creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: Why Can't Anyone Answer Questions About the Business? — Garrett Galow, WorkOS
videos.summary: WorkOS built Studio, an internal workspace where non-engineers can ask business questions against Snowflake, Linear, and Notion in natural language. The system generates reusable declarative JavaScript widgets that call data sources directly, enabling deterministic and cost-efficient query execution while avoiding repetitive manual requests.

videos.title: The agent-ready web: Simplify user actions with WebMCP — Tara Agyemang, Google
videos.summary: WebMCP is a proposed web standard introduced by Google that enables AI agents to interact with websites through structured tools instead of DOM parsing and pixel clicking. It provides both declarative HTML-based and imperative JavaScript-based APIs for registering actions, currently in early preview on Chrome 146.

videos.title: How to Keep Shipping When You Walk Away from Your Desk — Zack Proser, WorkOS
videos.summary: Zack Proser presents a four-layer stack for asynchronous agent-driven development: signal agents for Slack/Linear notifications, verification gates using lint and critic passes, weekly agent-driven conversation history review for skill generation, and Oura ring integration for well-being feedback. The core thesis is that human attention remains the bottleneck, not agent speed, and the solution involves voice-first coding with remote mobile oversight of agents dispatched to isolated git worktrees.

videos.title: Stop Making Models Bigger, Make Them Behave — Kobie Crawdord, Snorkel
videos.summary: A 4B model fine-tuned with reinforcement learning to use tools systematically outperformed a 235B model on database query tasks by learning tool discipline before reasoning. The approach achieved significant benchmark improvements on FinQA with training costs under $500.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 is Now BANNED?!
videos.summary: Cole Medin reviews Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 model, which was reportedly pulled from availability by US government export-control directive allegedly over jailbreak concerns. He compares its performance and pricing to Claude Opus 4.8 and discusses implications for developers building on these models.

videos.title: Google's Agents CLI: The CLI + Skills Combination to Ship AI Agents EASILY
videos.summary: Google released Agents CLI, an open-source tool that simplifies building and deploying AI agents to production. The tool provides agent identity, code sandboxing, audit trails, and integrates with AI coding assistants like Claude Code for rapid development and deployment.

videos.title: Live AI Q&A + Crushing it in Chess at the Same Time - Come Hang Out!
videos.summary: Cole Medin hosts a live AI Q&A session on Saturdays where viewers can ask questions about AI coding, agents, Claude Code, and Archon. The stream combines technical discussion with casual chess gameplay as part of community engagement for Dynamous.

videos.title: Claude Plans, Gemini Designs: The Workflow to Build BEAUTIFUL Frontends
videos.summary: Cole Medin demonstrates a workflow that routes planning and reasoning tasks to Claude Opus and design/frontend generation tasks to Gemini 3.5 Flash, optimizing both quality and cost. The approach chains these models through handoff documents and tooling to build full-stack applications end-to-end.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: The video explains how to build proactive AI agents that self-improve through automated loops. It covers the concept of AI loops, demonstrates use cases in autonomous ads optimization and SEO, and discusses tools like Loopany that enable memory and agent functionality.

videos.title: Ralph-loop 2.0? The real autonomous coder is coming...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses emerging autonomous coding features including the /goal feature and /Mission exploration for long horizontal tasks. The video covers how these features work, setup instructions, and goal-oriented prompting techniques in tools like Crewlet and Superdesign.

videos.title: New AI coding paradiagm - OpenAI Symphony
videos.summary: AI Jason presents OpenAI Symphony as a new AI coding paradigm. The video covers what Symphony is, how it works, harness setup with auto testing, and WORKFLOW.md configuration for Symphony setup.

videos.title: Okay, this unleashed my agent
videos.summary: A video overview of AI agent systems and self-evolving mechanisms, covering autoresearch frameworks, Claude's 3-layer memory architecture, Hermes self-evolution, and Openclaw skill comparison.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA released Nemotron 3 Nano, Super, and Ultra models that combine hybrid mamba transformer, mixture of experts, and MTP architectures. The video examines how these architectural innovations distinguish Nemotron 3 from other open-source models.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax released M3, a language model featuring Sparse Attention (MSA) that replaces full attention and includes tiled I/O improvements and optimized KV cache reading for inference efficiency. The model targets prefill and decode stage bottlenecks in infrastructure.

videos.title: Pi Agent explained in 6min..
videos.summary: Pi is an agent framework distinguished by its harness layer design that enables flexible integration patterns. The video compares Pi against frontier coding agents like Claude Code, Cursor, and Codex, examining how it scales as products add more complex features to agent architectures.

videos.title: Huawei makes 1.4nm chip??
videos.summary: Huawei announced Tau Scaling Law to compete in the hardware and semiconductor race between China and the USA. The video discusses how EUV and DUV lithography, along with packaging and logic folding, are key technologies shaping industry competition.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: AI and the Job Market in 2026
videos.summary: AWS Developers discusses job hunting strategy in an AI-saturated market, emphasizing networking over mass-applying, personal brand building, and the continued value of junior developers. The video covers the Feynman Principle for learning, open source contribution, building projects, and argues that AI will evolve jobs rather than eliminate them, using a transportation analogy.

videos.title: 5 Tips for Deploying AI Agents to Production
videos.summary: AWS Developers presents five production deployment patterns for AI agents built with TypeScript: streaming tool call events for UI responsiveness, separated auth layers using API Gateway and Cognito with IAM bridges, type-safe contracted database access with tenant isolation, guardrails with cost budgets and model routing, and observability via metrics and OpenTelemetry tracing. The patterns are demonstrated using Strands Agents SDK and AWS services.

videos.title: Infrastructure as Code for Beginners (AWS CDK)
videos.summary: Tutorial on Infrastructure as Code using AWS CDK with Python to build a serverless REST API. Covers building the same architecture manually via AWS console versus declaratively with CDK, then deploying, testing, and tearing down the entire infrastructure with single commands.

videos.title: Stop AI Hallucinations With These 5 Techniques
videos.summary: AWS Developers outlines five techniques to reduce AI hallucinations in agent systems: semantic tool selection to filter context, graph RAG for precise multi-hop queries, multi-agent validation for response verification, symbolic guardrails via Python code hooks, and runtime steering for self-correction. Demonstrations use Strands Agents framework with 85% token reduction in filtering examples.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: Alberta Tech examines whether Google engineers use AI-assisted code generation, comparing OpenAI Codex and Claude for code generation tasks. The video surveys vibing practices at Google across 2024-2026.

videos.title: This company killed open source
videos.summary: Alberta Tech discusses how an unnamed company is allegedly "killing" or ending open source development. The video features an interview with someone named Mike about Malus, a tool or service, and explores concerns around open source maintainers and sustainability.

videos.title: I let Claude Code take my job (it did better than me)
videos.summary: A developer tested Claude Code (Anthropic's coding assistant) on their own job tasks and reported that the AI performed better than manual work in some cases. The video explores workflow efficiency, terminal interactions, and raises questions about AI replacing software engineers.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: How to Steal Customers From Giants 600x Bigger than Your Startup | Pigment, Eléonore Crespo
videos.summary: Eléonore Crespo, co-founder of Pigment, discusses how to compete with much larger enterprise software vendors by building a full platform rather than single features, gaining trust through high-profile customer validation, and maintaining hiring standards. Pigment consolidates finance, supply chain, HR, and sales data for strategic decision-making.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, argues that passionate early adopters and formal customer interviews mislead founders. Real product-market fit manifests as customer pull rather than push, and success requires deep embedding in customer workflows while betting on AI model trajectory rather than current capabilities.

videos.title: He Raised $26M Betting on Probabilities, Not Guesses | Koah Labs, Nic Baird
videos.summary: Nic Baird, founder of Koah Labs, argues that ad-based monetization, not subscription models, will drive sustainable AI tool adoption. He contends that the current bottleneck for AI agents is not technological capability but the time required for humans to develop trust in AI systems.

videos.title: I Hit $1M ARR in 117 Days. Bootstrapped to $10M. Here's My Playbook | Chatbase, Yasser Elsaid
videos.summary: Yasser Elsaid, founder of Chatbase, shares his bootstrapping playbook for reaching $1M ARR in 117 days and scaling to $10M without external funding. The episode covers strategies on reducing churn, product-led growth, SEO and AEO tactics, outbound sales, pricing experiments, and decision-making frameworks for when to raise capital.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 Now Banned in Europe by US Government (EU-Breakdown)
videos.summary: A video by Cyril Imhof discusses the claimed ban of Claude Fable 5 in Europe by the US government. The video references regulatory developments concerning Anthropic's model access across EU jurisdictions.

videos.title: The Ultimate Second Brain for AI Entrepreneurs: Fable 5 Claude with Loops
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Fable 5, an Anthropic model designed for AI entrepreneurs, highlighting its features, guardrails, memory capabilities, and agentic payment integration compared to Mythos 5.

videos.title: Anthropic's Brand New AI Just Started the Gold Rush (Fable 5)
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Anthropic's new Claude Fable 5 model and its applications in regulated industries. The video covers new use cases for the model and introduces "Know Your Agent" methodology for effective context engineering in long-running AI applications.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
